mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which increases expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not positive in more than 50% of out-of-sample months, or the t-statistic of the mean return is below 1.0.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 25 basis points signals a shift to restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS has risen > 0.25pp in the prior 2 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated unemployment and a rising federal funds rate enforce a joint tightening of financial conditions and household income that depresses corporate earnings expectations more than either factor alone, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE > 6.0% and FEDFUNDS > 1.5%, the S&P 500 delivers negative 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or the interaction term in a bivariate regression has a p-value > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest-rate environment, month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand that directly lifts revenue expectations for consumer-facing equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%, positive month-over-month RSAFS growth is not associated with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of subsequent months.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month contraction in industrial production signals deteriorating manufacturing activity and capacity under-utilization, which reduces earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances following a 1.0% two-month decline in INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) confirm a broad-based demand recovery that lifts cyclical revenues and reduces recession risk, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where both HOUST > 900 and RSAFS > 350000, the S&P 500 does not deliver positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or the joint interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment exceeds 5.5%, accelerating payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals diminishing labor slack and stronger wage-driven consumption, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, month-over-month PAYEMS increases do not precede positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 58% of out-of-sample observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) above its trailing four-quarter average signals a demand acceleration that reduces recession probability and lifts broad equity indices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarters with accelerating GDPC1 growth do not coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations, or the mean forward return is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).
